description_long: This is a single-bladed axe with a dark, roughly textured metal head and a distinctively curved wooden handle. The blade is broad and asymmetrical, featuring a slightly irregular edge and visible surface markings that suggest wear or hand-forging. The handle is medium-length, with a pronounced bend near the lower end, likely designed for ergonomic grip and leverage. The axe head is mounted onto the handle in a traditional style, with the metal passing through the top of the wood. The overall appearance is rugged and utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty tasks such as chopping wood or demolition. The coloration is predominantly dark brown and black, with a matte, slightly rough finish.
description: A rugged, single-bladed axe with a broad, dark metal head and a curved wooden handle, featuring a worn, hand-forged look.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top, showing the narrow profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the broad, irregularly shaped blade and the pronounced curve of the handle.
description_view_2: The axe is viewed from the bottom, displaying the pointed end of the blade and the slim handle.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals the full expanse of the blade and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a pronounced bend, oval cross-section, and a grippy, unfinished texture.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, asymmetrical single blade with a slightly irregular edge and visible surface markings; no beard or poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted through the top of the wooden handle.
